item.title: Did Trump Just Threaten to Use Nuclear Weapons in Iran?
item.description: If such an order comes to pass, the military can and must refuse.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: Trump Looks Frantic
item.description: So much bluster signifying nothing
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: Trump Threatens to Destroy an Entire Nation
item.description: The president’s position is that if he wants to wipe out a “whole civilization,” then that is his decision to make.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The Quintessential Trumpian Sport
item.description: Though despised by the American right, soccer is a clear expression of the president’s politics.
item.dc:creator: Franklin Foer

item.title: The Hormuz War Will End
item.description: And when it does, a rare window for bold collective action will open.
item.dc:creator: Amos Hochstein

item.title: Moon Joy: Photos From Artemis II
item.description: On April 6, 2026, four astronauts aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft, Integrity, swung around the far side of the moon, traveling farther from the Earth than any humans had ever gone before, and taking spectacular photographs along the way.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Why Latinos Join ICE
item.description: One reason: They’re aiming to protect, not betray, their communities.
item.dc:creator: Geraldo L. Cadava

item.title: The $97 Million Utah Warehouse ICE Bought for $145 Million
item.description: Trump wants industrial-size immigration jails, and money is no obstacle.
item.dc:creator: Nick Miroff

item.title: Trump’s Fundamental Misunderstanding in Iran
item.description: What makes the nation suffer helps the regime thrive.
item.dc:creator: Karim Sadjadpour

item.title: How the Whole-Grain Trend Went Wrong
item.description: Many whole-grain foods behave in the body much the same as the refined products they were meant to replace.
item.dc:creator: Trisha Pasricha

item.title: The Feeling of Becoming Less and Less of a Person
item.description: In Ben Lerner’s new novel, technology divides us further from one another, and ourselves.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Dames

item.title: Three Things the Consensus Gets Wrong About the Iran War
item.description: Things might not be as bleak as they are generally portrayed.
item.dc:creator: Eliot A. Cohen

item.title: What Happens When Trump Feels Cornered
item.description: The president’s most inflammatory remarks tend to come when he gets frustrated—which might explain his recent outbursts.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The ChatGPT Symptom Spiral
item.description: Be careful asking chatbots about your health.
item.dc:creator: Sage Lazzaro

item.title: This Is Not How Presidents Typically Communicate
item.description: In an Easter Sunday blast, Donald Trump broke with the way his predecessors comported themselves during times of war.
item.dc:creator: Barbara A. Perry

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: What Demonym Is That?
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Savannah Guthrie and the Hard Truth About True Crime
item.description: Everyone wants a happy outcome. But sometimes, the greatest relief is any ending at all.
item.dc:creator: Megan Garber

item.title: Venezuela Seems to Be Going … Well?
item.description: The administration’s plan is working, but democracy remains elusive.
item.dc:creator: Missy Ryan

item.title: Wrestling’s Newest Star Is Massive, Bearded, and Ready to Piledrive ICE
item.description: The debate over immigration enforcement has crept into a brash and crass entertainment, which is less immune to reality than you’d think.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Gordon

item.title: What Happened After a Teacher Ditched Screens
item.description: Why one early adopter of computers in classrooms has decided to toss them
item.dc:creator: Jenny Anderson
